description: You are a QA technician at an ISO-certified commercial vehicle workshop. A 2016 Fiat Ducato 2.3 Multijet 130HP has returned 72 hours after major repair work (turbo actuator replacement and body computer software reflash). The customer reports intermittent hesitation and a warning light that appeared once. Perform a formal post-repair multi-system verification using Multiecuscan in simulation mode. Scan the Engine ECU and the Body Computer, recording ECU identification (part numbers, HW/SW versions), all stored and pending DTCs, and at least eight engine parameters. Cross-reference all findings against the reference data files in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanData\: every DTC against dtc_database_full.csv for its full description, each engine parameter against normal idle ranges in obd2_parameter_reference.csv, and the vehicle engine code and ECU type against fiat_vehicle_specs.csv. Write a formal QA verification report to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\qa_verification_report.txt containing per-system findings with cross-referenced DTC descriptions, a parameter comparison table (observed values vs. normal ranges from CSV), vehicle specification verification, and a QA verdict of PASSED, CONDITIONAL PASS, or FAILED with supporting reasoning. The QA work order on the Desktop has full vehicle details and repair history.
natural_language: After turbo and body computer repairs on a Fiat Ducato, perform post-repair QA verification: scan Engine ECU and Body Computer in Multiecuscan simulation mode, then cross-reference all DTCs against dtc_database_full.csv and all engine parameters against obd2_parameter_reference.csv normal ranges. Verify vehicle specs in fiat_vehicle_specs.csv. Write a formal QA report with cross-referenced findings and a PASSED/CONDITIONAL/FAILED verdict.
